{{Short description|Men's association football team}}
{{Distinguish|DR Congo national football team}}
{{About|the men's team|the women's team|Congo women's national football team}}
{{Infobox national football team
| Name              = Congo
| Badge             = 
| Badge_size        = 190px 
| Nickname          = {{lang|fr|Diables Rouges}} (The Red Devils) <br> {{lang|fr|Guerriers de l'Équateur}} (Warriors of the [[Equator]])
| Association       = [[Congolese Football Federation|Fédération Congolaise de Football]] (FECOFOOT)
| Sub-confederation = [[Central African Football Federations' Union|UNIFFAC]] (Central Africa)
| Confederation     = [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]] (Africa)
| Coach             = [[Claude Le Roy]] 
| Captain           = [[Béranger Itoua]]
| Most caps         = [[Jonas Bahamboula]] <br /> [[Delvin N'Dinga]] (56)
| Top scorer        = [[Thievy Bifouma]] (16)
| Home Stadium      = [[Stade Municipal de Kintélé|Stade ya mbanza Kintélé]]<br />[[Stade Alphonse Massemba-Débat]]
| FIFA Trigramme    = CGO
| FIFA Rank         = {{FIFA World Rankings|CGO}}
| FIFA max          = 42
| FIFA max date     = September 2015
| FIFA min          = 144
| FIFA min date     = September 2011
| Elo Rank          = {{World Football Elo Ratings|Congo}}
| Elo max           = 37
| Elo max date      = July 1972
| Elo min           = 133
| Elo min date      = 4 September 2011
| pattern_la1       = _cgo2021h
| pattern_b1        = _cgo22h
| pattern_ra1       = _cgo2021h
| pattern_sh1       = 
| leftarm1          = ed0000
| body1             = ed0000
| rightarm1         = ed0000
| shorts1           = FF0000
| socks1            = FF0000
| pattern_la2       = _cgo2021t
| pattern_b2        = _cgo22a
| pattern_ra2       = _cgo2021t
| pattern_sh2       = _cgo22a
| pattern_so2       = _cgo22a
| leftarm2          = FFFFFF
| body2             = FFFFFF
| rightarm2         = FFFFFF
| shorts2           = ffffff
| socks2            = ffffff
| pattern_b3        = _cgo22t
| pattern_la3       = _cgo22t
| pattern_ra3       = _cgo22t
| pattern_sh3       = 
| socks3            = FFF200
| First game         =  {{flagicon|FRA|1830}} '''French Congo''' 5–1 [[Cameroon national football team|Cameroon]] {{flagicon|FRA|1830}}<br />([[Middle Congo]]; 1954)<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.eloratings.net/Congo|title=World Football Elo Ratings|website=www.eloratings.net|accessdate=6 April 2023|archive-date=9 August 2022|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220809162945/http://www.eloratings.net/Congo|url-status=live}}</ref>
| Largest win       = {{nowrap|{{fb|Congo}} 11–0 {{fb-rt|CHA}} <br /> ([[Democratic Republic of the Congo|Congo]]; 28 March 1964) <br /> {{fb|CGO|1970}} 11–0 {{fb-rt|STP}} <br /> ([[Libreville]], [[Gabon]]; 7 July 1976)}}
| Largest loss      = {{nowrap|{{fb|MAD|name=Malagasy Republic}} 8–1 {{fb-rt|CGO}} }}<br /> ([[Antananarivo]], [[Madagascar]]; 18 April 1960)
| Regional name     = [[Africa Cup of Nations]]
| Regional cup apps = 7
| Regional cup first= [[1968 African Cup of Nations|1968]]
| Regional cup best = '''Champions''' ([[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]])
|2ndRegional cup apps=
|2ndRegional name=
|2ndRegional cup first=
|2ndRegional cup best=
|pattern_so1=_cgo22h|pattern_so3=_cgo22t|leftarm3=FFF200|body3=FFF200|rightarm3=FFF200|shorts3=FFF200}}

The '''Congo national football team''' ({{langx|fr|Équipe du Congo de football}}) represents the [[Republic of the Congo]] in men's [[association football]] and is governed by the [[Congolese Football Federation]]. They have never qualified for the [[FIFA World Cup|World Cup]], but did win the [[Africa Cup of Nations]] in [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]. They also won the [[All-Africa Games]] football tournament in [[1965 All-Africa Games|1965]]. The team is also a member of both [[FIFA]] and the [[Confederation of African Football]] (CAF).

== History ==
The Congo national football team made its first ever appearance in February 1960 in a [[Exhibition game|friendly]] against the [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]] which they lost 4–2.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.rsssf.org/tablesk/kong-intres.html|title=Congo (Brazzaville) - List of International Matches|website=[[RSSSF]]|publisher=Rec. Sport Soccer Statistics Foundation|date=23 December 2013|access-date=29 January 2014|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20190819195957/http://www.rsssf.com/tablesk/kong-intres.html|archive-date=19 August 2019|url-status=live}}</ref> On 13 April, they defeated [[Réunion national football team|Reunion]] 4–1 in their first game to advance to the quarter-finals. In their quarter-final on 15 April, they defeated the [[Ivory Coast national football team|Ivory Coast]] 3–2. On 17 April, they lost 5–4 to [[Cameroon national football team|Cameroon]] and were beaten 8–1 by the host [[Madagascar national football team|Madagascar]] in the third-place play-off on 19 April.

In April 1963 they entered another ''L'Amitié'' competition, this time in [[Senegal]], and were drawn in a group with [[Tunisia national football team|Tunisia]], the Ivory Coast, [[Congo-Kinshasa national football team|Democratic Republic of Congo]] and [[Mauritania national football team|Mauritania]]. They lost their opener 2–0 to Tunisia on 13 April but beat the Ivory Coast 3–2 the next day. On 15 April they beat their neighbour Congo Kinshasa 2–1, and then Mauritania 11–0 two days later, but did not advance to the next round.

In July 1965 the Congo held the [[1965 All-Africa Games]] and were drawn in a group with [[Mali national football team|Mali]], [[Uganda national football team|Uganda]] and [[Togo national football team|Togo]]. They drew 1–1 with Mali on 18 July and beat Uganda 2–1 the next day. On 21 July they drew 1–1 against Togo but advanced through to the semi-finals, where they beat the Ivory Coast 1–0 on 23 July. On 25 July the Congo drew 0–0 versus Mali in the final, but won the tournament by having won ten corners in the final compared to Mali's one.

On 11 January 1967 the Congo played their first non-African opposition, defeating [[Romania national football team|Romania]] 1–0 in a home friendly. On 19 February 1967 the Congo travelled to [[Tunisia]] for their first ever African Cup of Nations qualifier, drawing 1–1. On 2 August 1967 they hosted a qualifier against Cameroon, and defeated them 2–1 to top their qualifying group and advance to their first finals.

The finals were [[1968 African Cup of Nations|held in Ethiopia in January 1968]] and the Congo were drawn in a group with their neighbour [[Zaire national football team|Zaire]], [[Senegal national football team|Senegal]] and [[Ghana national football team|Ghana]]. They lost the opener to Zaire 3–0 on 12 January and two days later lost 2–1 to Senegal. On 16 January the Congo were defeated 3–1 by Ghana and were knocked out.

The Congo hosted a friendly against Romania for the second successive year on 16 June 1968 and won 4–2. On 30 July 1968 they played their first ever South American opposition, losing a home friendly 2–0 to [[Brazil national football team|Brazil]].

In [[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]], the Congo won their only [[African Cup of Nations]] title. Congo defeated host [[Cameroon national football team|Cameroon]] in the semi-final 1–0 before beating [[Mali national football team|Mali]] 3–2 to claim the championship. On that squad was arguably Congo's most famous player, [[François M'Pelé]], who starred for [[Paris Saint-Germain F.C.|PSG]] in the 1970s.

In qualification for the [[1998 World Cup]], the Congo came within a win of qualifying for the final tournament. However, after home wins over [[Zambia national football team|Zambia]], [[DR Congo national football team|DR Congo]] and [[South Africa national football team|South Africa]], Congo lost their final match 1–0 away to South Africa and was eliminated.

In February 2025 Congo was suspended from international competitions by FIFA due to "third-party interference" in [[Congolese Football Federation|FECOFOOT]] - Congolese football's governing body.<ref>{{cite web |title=FIFA suspends the Congolese Football Association and the Pakistan Football Federation |url=https://inside.fifa.com/organisation/media-releases/fifa-suspends-congolese-football-association-pakistan-football-federation |website=FIFA.com |publisher=FIFA |access-date=11 April 2025}}</ref>

== Results and fixtures ==
{{main|Congo national football team results (2020–present)}}

The following is a list of match results in the last 12 months, as well as any future matches that have been scheduled.

{{legend2|#CCFFCC|Win|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFCC|Draw|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFCCCC|Loss|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}
{{legend2|#FFFFFF|Fixture|border=1px solid #AAAAAA}}

=== 2025 ===
{{Footballbox collapsible
| format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 5 September
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|CGO}}
| score      =1–1 
| report     =https://www.bbc.com/sport/football/live/c7vl2jqplggt 
| team2      = {{fb|TAN}}
| goals1     =[[Dechan Moussavou|Moussavou]] {{goal|68}} 
| goals2     =[[Mbwana Samatta|Samatta]] {{goal|84}} 
| stadium    = [[Stade Alphonse Massemba-Débat]]
| location   = [[Brazzaville]], Congo
| attendance = 
| referee    =Thabang Ketshabile ([[Botswana Football Association|Botwsana]]) 
| result     =D 
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 8 September
| time       = 
| team1      = {{fb-rt|CGO}}
| score      = Canceled
| report     = 
| team2      = {{fb|ERI}}
| goals1     = 
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = 
| location   = TBD, Congo
| attendance = 
| referee    = 
| result     = V
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 8 October
| time       = {{UTZ|19:00|0}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|NIG}}
| score      = 3–1
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021055
| team2      = {{fb|CGO}}
| goals1     = *[[Daniel Sosah|Sosah]] {{goal|45+2}}
*[[Youssouf Oumarou|Oumarou]] {{goal|49}}
*[[Victorien Adebayor|Adebayor]] {{goal|67}}
| goals2     = [[Déo Bassinga|Bassinga]] {{goal|90+4}}
| stadium    = [[Stade du 4 Août]]
| location   = [[Ouagadougou]], [[Burkina Faso]]
| attendance = 
| referee    = Boubou Traoré ([[Malian Football Federation|Mali]])
| result     = L 
}}
{{Footballbox collapsible
| format=1
| round      = [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]]
| date       = 13 October
| time       = {{UTZ|20:00|1}}
| team1      = {{fb-rt|MAR}}
| score      = 1–0
| report     = https://www.fifa.com/en/match-centre/match/520/288282/288289/400021057
| team2      = {{fb|CGO}}
| goals1     = [[Youssef En-Nesyri|En-Nesyri]] {{goal|63}}
| goals2     = 
| stadium    = [[Prince Moulay Abdellah Stadium]]
| location   = [[Rabat]], Morocco
| attendance = 
| referee    = Abdel Aziz Bouh ([[Football Federation of the Islamic Republic of Mauritania|Mauritania]])
| result     = L
}}

=== 2026 ===
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CGO}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|COM}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Congo
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|COM}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|CGO}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Comoros
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CGO}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|NAM}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Congo
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|NAM}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|CGO}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Namibia
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CMR}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|CGO}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Cameroon
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}
{{footballbox collapsible
|format = 1
|round = [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations qualification|2027 AFCON qualification]]
|date = TBD
|time = TBD
|team1 = {{fb-rt|CGO}}
|score =
|team2 = {{fb|CMR}}
|report =
|goals1 =
|goals2 =
|location = TBD, Congo
|stadium = TBD
|attendance =
|referee =
|result =
}}

==Coaching staff==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Position
! Name
|-
|Head coach
|{{flagicon|FRA}} [[Claude Le Roy]]
|-
|Assistant coach
|{{flagicon|SEN}} [[Omar Daf]]
|-
|Goalkeeping coach
|{{flagicon|CGO}} Gérald Mbandaka
|-
|Fitness coach
|{{flagicon|CGO}} Formose Kimbuta
|-
|Match analyst
|{{flagicon|CGO}} François Mpanga
|-
|Doctors
|{{flagicon|CGO}} Dr. Stéphane Mavungu<br />{{flagicon|CGO}} Dr. Gervais Tchibinda
|-
|Physiotherapists
|{{flagicon|CGO}} Aurélien Kimpembe<br />{{flagicon|CGO}} Maurice Mvoungou<br />{{flagicon|CGO}} Loïc Kimbela<br />{{flagicon|CGO}} Jérôme Tchuka
|-
|Team coordinator
|{{flagicon|CGO}} Hervé Ngolo
|-
|Technical director
|{{flagicon|CGO}} Maxime Nzassi
|}

=== Coaching history ===
{{columns-list|colwidth=20em|
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Paul Ebondzibato]] (1962–1964)
* {{flagicon|USSR}} [[Vasily Sokolov (footballer)|Vasily Sokolov]] (1964–1965)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Paul Ebondzibato]] (1965–1970)
* {{flagicon|CGO|1970}} [[Adolphe Bibanzoulou]] (1970–1973)
* {{flagicon|CGO|1970}} [[Robert Ndoudi]] (1973–1974)
* {{flagicon|ROU|1965}} [[Cicerone Manolache]] (1974–1976)
* {{flagicon|YUG}} [[Zoran Ristić]] (1984)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Yvon Goujon]] (1986–1987)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Noël Minga]] (1992–1993, 2001)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[David Mémy]] (1997–1998)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Alain Nestor Ngouinda]] (1998–1999)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[David Mémy]] (1999–2000)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Camille Ngakosso]] (2000)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Gaston Tchangana]] (2001, 2005–2006, 2007–2008)
* {{flagicon|ROM}} [[Eugen Moldovan]] (2001–2002)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Alain Nestor Ngouinda]] (2002)
* {{flagicon|SUI}} [[Claude Andrey]] (2002–2003)
* {{flagicon|NED}} [[Tahseen Jabbary]] (2002–2003)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Jean-Paul Bernard]] (2003)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Christian Létard]] (2004–2005)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Noël Tosi]] (2006–2007)
* {{flagicon|SRB|2004}} [[Ivica Todorov]] (2008–2010)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Robert Corfou]] (2010–2011)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Camille Ngakosso]] (2011)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Jean-Guy Wallemme]] (2011–2012)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Kamel Djabour]] (2012–2013)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Claude Le Roy]] (2013–2015)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Pierre Lechantre]] (2016)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Barthélémy Ngatsono]] (2016–2017)
* {{flagicon|FRA|1974}} [[Sébastien Migné]] (2017–2018)
* {{flagicon|BRA}} [[Valdo Filho]] (2018–2021)
* {{flagicon|Belgium}} [[Paul Put]] (2021–2023)
* {{flagicon|FRA}} [[Isaac Ngata]] (2023–2025)
* {{flagicon|CGO}} [[Barthélémy Ngatsono]] (2025)
* {{flagicon|ITA}} [[Fabrizio Cesana]] (2025)
* {{flagicon|FRA}} [[Claude Le Roy]] (2026–Present)
}}

== Players ==

=== Current squad ===
The following players were called-up for the [[2026 FIFA World Cup qualification – CAF Group E|2026 FIFA World Cup qualification]] matches against [[Niger national football team|Niger]] and [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]] on 8 and 13 October 2025, respectively.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.facebook.com/fecofootcg/posts/pfbid029FLWkxXKB7eqHfbCmuAPrCA13w54pBH7yxVQqNmZUbfMXKY8gG3jMDCVWx5pMaAnl|title=Le sélectionneur Fabrizio Cesana a convoqué 25 joueurs  pour les deux prochaines affiches des Diables Rouges|trans-title=Head coach Fabrizio Cesana has called up 25 players for the Red Devils' next two matches|publisher=[[Congolese Football Federation]]|date=30 September 2025|language=fr|via=[[Facebook]]}}</ref>

''Caps and goals are correct as of 13 October 2025, after the match against [[Morocco national football team|Morocco]]'' <!-- ATTENTION: substitutes for friendly match against Gabon still unknown-->

{{nat fs g start}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Chelcy Bonazebi]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|5|18|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=Red Star|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Ulrich Samba]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|7|1|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Perrauld Ndinga]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|5|8|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AS Mangasport]]|clubnat=GAB}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Béranger Itoua]]|age={{birth date and age|1992|5|9|df=y}}|caps=20|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Prince Mouandza]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|10|23|df=y}}|caps=12|goals=1|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Romaric Etou]]|age={{birth date and age|1995|1|25|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[FC Dila Gori|Dila Gori]]|clubnat=GEO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Berlohd Mbemba]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|3|6|df=y}}|caps=10|goals=0|club=[[Al Merrikh SC|Al Merrikh]]|clubnat=SDN}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Christ Makosso]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|5|9|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[Oxford United F.C.|Oxford United]]|clubnat=ENG}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Charles Atipo]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|12|28|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[John Kapaya]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|9|3|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[AC Léopards]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Pachoud De Nzingoula]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|9|4|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AC Léopards]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Merveille Ndockyt]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|7|20|df=y}}|caps=28|goals=1|club=[[HNK Rijeka|Rijeka]]|clubnat=CRO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Chandrel Massanga]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|8|17|df=y}}|caps=13|goals=1|club=[[Hatayspor]]|clubnat=TUR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Gédéon Nongo]]|age={{birth date and age|2007|12|27|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Glid Otanga]]|age={{birth date and age|2000|8|14|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Bnei Sakhnin F.C.|Bnei Sakhnin]]|clubnat=ISR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Venold Dzaba]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|5|18|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[AC Léopards]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Inno Loemba]]|age={{birth date and age|1998|8|4|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Colombe Sportive]]|clubnat=CMR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Dericka Ossebi]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|5|31|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[AC Léopards]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Junior Tchibinda]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|4|5|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[BFC Daugavpils|Daugavpils]]|clubnat=LVA}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Déo Bassinga]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|8|11|df=y}}|caps=9|goals=1|club=[[Fath Union Sport|FUS Rabat]]|clubnat=MAR}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Dechan Moussavou]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|8|7|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=2|club=[[AC Léopards]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Wilfrid Nkaya]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|9|17|df=y}}|caps=6|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Ismaël Ankobo]]|age={{birth date and age|1997|10|13|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Power Dynamos F.C.|Power Dynamos]]|clubnat=ZAM}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Mignon Koto]]|age={{birth date and age|1999|9|17|df=y}}|caps=5|goals=0|club=[[Masfout Club|Masfout]]|clubnat=UAE}}
{{nat fs g player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Carly Ekongo]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|3|24|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=1|club=[[AC Léopards]]|clubnat=CGO}}
{{nat fs g end}}

=== Recent call-ups ===
The following players were called up for Congo in the last 12 months.
{{nat fs r start}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Christophe Wamba]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|9|16|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Étoile du Congo]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 5 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=GK|name=[[Dhody Foutou]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|12|10|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[CSMD Diables Noirs|Diables Noirs]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Pedro Peya]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|19|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[Abu Salim SC|Abu Salim]]|clubnat=LBY|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 5 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Djigo Saïkou]]|age={{birth date and age|2004|11|20|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[MB Rouissat]]|clubnat=ALG|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 5 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Van-Igor Boukaka]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|6|26|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Inter Club Brazzaville|Inter Club]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=DF|name=[[Vicalor Odzalambaye]]|age=|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[Vita Club Mokanda|Vita Club]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Grâce Nsemi]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|6|15|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 5 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Digne Pounga]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|5|15|df=y}}|caps=1|goals=0|club=[[Atalanta BC Under-23|Atalanta U23]]|clubnat=ITA|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 5 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Brudet Okana]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|3|30|df=y}}|caps=4|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Hergie Mossala]]|age={{birth date and age|2003|3|26|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[AC Léopards]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=MF|name=[[Gosim Elenga]]|age={{birth date and age|2001|6|23|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[CSMD Diables Noirs|Diables Noirs]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Grâce Mavoungou]]|age={{birth date and age|2008|3|18|df=y}}|caps=3|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 5 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Japhet Mankou]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|6|7|df=y}}|caps=8|goals=1|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=v. {{fb|TAN}}, 5 September 2025}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Elie Andzouono]]|age={{birth date and age|2002|1|2|df=y}}|caps=7|goals=0|club=[[AS Otohô]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Chadrack Ngantsui]]|age={{birth date and age|2006|5|28|df=y}}|caps=2|goals=0|club=[[Étoile du Congo]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs r player|no=|pos=FW|name=[[Carly Nongo]]|age={{birth date and age|2005|3|24|df=y}}|caps=0|goals=0|club=[[AS Cheminots]]|clubnat=CGO|latest=[[2024 African Nations Championship|CHAN 2024]]}}
{{nat fs break}}
*<sup>DEC</sup> Player refused to join the team after the call-up.
*<sup>INJ</sup> Player withdrew from the squad due to an injury.
*<sup>PRE</sup> Preliminary squad.
*<sup>RET</sup> Player has retired from international football.
*<sup>SUS</sup> Suspended from the national team.
{{nat fs end|background=}}

== Records ==
{{Updated|14 October 2025}}<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/kongb-recintlp.html |title=Congo-Brazzaville&nbsp;– Record International Players |author=Mamrud, Roberto |website=[[RSSSF]] |access-date=8 March 2018 |archive-date=10 July 2022 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20220710163314/https://www.rsssf.org/miscellaneous/kongb-recintlp.html |url-status=live }}</ref>
:''Players in '''bold''' are still active with Congo.''

=== Most appearances ===
[[File:Locomotiv-Terec (6).jpg|175px|right|thumb|[[Delvin N'Dinga]] is Congo's joint-most capped player with 56 appearances.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Goals
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| rowspan=2|1 ||align=left| [[Jonas Bahamboula]] || 56 || 13 || 1969–1982
|-
|align=left| [[Delvin N'Dinga]]|| 56 || 1 || 2008–2021
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[Destin Makita]] || 55 || 1 || 2001–2013
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Barel Mouko]] || 51 || 1 || 2004–2018
|-
| 5 ||align=left| [[Dimitri Magnoléké Bissiki|Magnoléké Bissiki]] || 45 || 0 || 2012–2021
|-
| 6 ||align=left| [[Oscar Ewolo]] || 44 || 2 || 2000–2013
|-
| 7 ||align=left| [[Prince Oniangué]] || 43 || 8 || 2008–2019
|-
| rowspan=2|8 ||align=left| [[Thievy Bifouma]] || 41 || 16 || 2014–2023
|-
|align=left| [[Francis N'Ganga]] || 41 || 3 || 2008–2017
|-
| rowspan=2|10 ||align=left| '''[[Christoffer Mafoumbi]]''' || 38 || 0 || 2012–present
|-
|align=left| [[Brice Samba (footballer, born 1971)|Brice Samba]] || 38 || 0 || 1990–2001
|-
|}

=== Top goalscorers ===
[[File:Thievy Bifouma (Reims).JPG|125px|right|thumb|[[Thievy Bifouma]] is Congo's top scorer with 16 goals.]]

{| class="wikitable sortable" style="text-align:center;"
|-
!width=30px|Rank
! style="width:150px;"|Player
!width=50px|Goals
!width=50px|Caps
!width=50px|Ratio
! style="width:100px;"|Career
|-
| 1 ||align=left| [[Thievy Bifouma]] || 16 || 41 || {{#expr:16/41 round 2}} || 2014–2023
|-
| 2 ||align=left| [[Jonas Bahamboula]] || 13 || 56 || {{#expr:13/56 round 2}} || 1969–1982
|-
| 3 ||align=left| [[François M'Pelé]] || 12 || 29 || {{#expr:12/29 round 2}} || 1971–1978
|-
| 4 ||align=left| [[Paul Moukila]] || 11 || 31 || {{#expr:11/31 round 2}} || 1970–1978
|-
| rowspan=2|5 ||align=left| [[Anges Ngapy]] || 10 || 33 || {{#expr:10/33 round 2}} || 1984–1993
|-
|align=left| [[Férébory Doré]] || 10 || 37 || {{#expr:10/37 round 2}} || 2010–2017
|-
| rowspan=2|7 ||align=left| [[Jean-Jacques N'Domba]] || 8 || 35 || {{#expr:8/35 round 2}} || 1974–1992
|-
|align=left| [[Prince Oniangué]] || 8 || 43 || {{#expr:8/43 round 2}} || 2008–2019
|-
| rowspan=6|9 ||align=left| [[Kader Bidimbou]] || 5 || 13 || {{#expr:5/13 round 2}} || 2014–2023
|-
|align=left| [[Guy Mbenza]] || 5 || 21 || {{#expr:5/21 round 2}} || 2017–2023
|-
|align=left| [[Rolf-Christel Guié-Mien]] || 5 || 25 || {{#expr:5/25 round 2}} || 1996–2008
|-
|align=left| [[Hardy Binguila]] || 5 || 29 || {{#expr:5/29 round 2}} || 2013–2023
|-
|align=left| '''[[Silvère Ganvoula]]''' || 5 || 29 || {{#expr:5/29 round 2}} || 2014–present
|-
|align=left| [[Fabrice Ondama]] || 5 || 37 || {{#expr:5/37 round 2}} || 2006–2017
|-
|}

== Competition records ==

=== FIFA World Cup ===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[FIFA World Cup]] record
!rowspan=3|
!colspan=6|[[FIFA World Cup qualification|Qualification]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}*
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|[[1930 FIFA World Cup|1930]] to [[1958 FIFA World Cup|1958]]
|colspan=8 |''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|colspan=6 |''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
!colspan=16|as '''{{fb|CGO|name=Congo-Brazzaville}}'''
|-
| {{flagicon|Chile}} [[1962 FIFA World Cup|1962]]
|colspan=8|''Not a FIFA member''
!rowspan=2|
|colspan=6|''Not a FIFA member''
|-
|{{flagicon|England}} [[1966 FIFA World Cup|1966]]
|Colspan=8|''Entry not accepted by FIFA''
|Colspan=6|''Entry not accepted by FIFA''
|-
!colspan=16|as '''{{fb|CGO|1970|name=People's Republic of the Congo}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1970 FIFA World Cup|1970]]
|Colspan=8|''Did not enter''
!rowspan=6|
|Colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
|{{flagicon|West Germany}} [[1974 FIFA World Cup|1974]]
|Rowspan=2 Colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|2
|0
|1
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|Argentina}} [[1978 FIFA World Cup|1978]]
|4
|1
|1
|2
|7
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Spain}} [[1982 FIFA World Cup|1982]]
|rowspan=3 Colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|rowspan=3 Colspan=6|''Did not enter''
|-
| {{flagicon|Mexico}} [[1986 FIFA World Cup|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Italy}} [[1990 FIFA World Cup|1990]]
|-
!colspan=16|as '''{{fb|CGO|name=Republic of the Congo}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|United States}} [[1994 FIFA World Cup|1994]]
|rowspan=9 Colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
!rowspan=12|
|4
|0
|0
|4
|0
|5
|-
|{{flagicon|France|1974}} [[1998 FIFA World Cup|1998]]
|8
|4
|2
|2
|8
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|South Korea|1997}} {{flagicon|Japan}} [[2002 FIFA World Cup|2002]]
|10
|3
|2
|5
|10
|17
|-
|{{flagicon|Germany}} [[2006 FIFA World Cup|2006]]
|12
|4
|2
|6
|12
|15
|-
|{{flagicon|South Africa}} [[2010 FIFA World Cup|2010]]
|6
|3
|0
|3
|7
|8
|-
|{{flagicon|Brazil}} [[2014 FIFA World Cup|2014]]
|8
|4
|3
|1
|13
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|Russia}} [[2018 FIFA World Cup|2018]]
|8
|2
|2
|4
|11
|16
|-
|{{flagicon|Qatar}} [[2022 FIFA World Cup|2022]]
|6
|0
|3
|3
|5
|10
|- 
|{{flagicon|Canada}} {{flagicon|Mexico}} {{flagicon|United States}} [[2026 FIFA World Cup|2026]]
|8
|0
|1
|7
|4
|24
|-
|{{flagicon|Morocco}} {{flagicon|Portugal}} {{flagicon|Spain}} [[2030 FIFA World Cup|2030]]
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''To be determined''
|rowspan=2 colspan=8|''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|Saudi Arabia}} [[2034 FIFA World Cup|2034]]
|-
!Total
!
!{{Tooltip|0/15|Number of tournaments qualified for}}
!
!
!
!
!
!
!76
!21
!17
!38
!79
!116
|}

=== Africa Cup of Nations ===
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
!colspan=9|[[Africa Cup of Nations]] record
|-
!Year
!Round
!Position
!{{Tooltip|Pld|Games played}}
!{{Tooltip|W|Won}}
!{{Tooltip|D|Drawn}}
!{{Tooltip|L|Lost}}
!{{Tooltip|GF|Goals for}}
!{{Tooltip|GA|Goals against}}
|-
|{{flagicon|Sudan|1956}} [[1957 African Cup of Nations|1957]]||colspan=8 rowspan=2|''Part of {{fb|France}}''
|-
|{{flagicon|United Arab Republic}} [[1959 African Cup of Nations|1959]]
|-
|colspan=9|Played as '''{{fb|CGO|name=Congo-Brazzaville}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ethiopia|1897}} [[1962 Africa Cup of Nations|1962]]||rowspan=3 colspan=8|''Not affiliated to CAF''
|-
|{{flagicon|Ghana}} [[1963 Africa Cup of Nations|1963]]
|-
|{{flagicon|Tunisia|1959}} [[1965 Africa Cup of Nations|1965]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1897}} [[1968 Africa Cup of Nations|1968]]
|Group stage
|7th
|3
|0
|0
|3
|2
|8
|-
|colspan=9|Played as '''{{fb|CGO|1970|name=People's Republic of the Congo}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|SDN|1956}} [[1970 Africa Cup of Nations|1970]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|- style="background:gold;"
|{{flagicon|CMR|1961}} [[1972 Africa Cup of Nations|1972]]
|'''Winners'''
|'''1st'''
|'''5'''
|'''3'''
|'''1'''
|'''1'''
|'''9'''
|'''7'''
|- style="background:#9ACDFF;"
|{{flagicon|EGY|1972}} [[1974 Africa Cup of Nations|1974]]
|'''Fourth place'''
|'''4th'''
|'''5'''
|'''2'''
|'''1'''
|'''2'''
|'''7'''
|'''10'''
|-
|{{flagicon|ETH|1975}} [[1976 Africa Cup of Nations|1976]]
|colspan=8|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[1978 Africa Cup of Nations|1978]]
|Group stage
|7th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|1
|4
|-
|{{flagicon|NGA}} [[1980 Africa Cup of Nations|1980]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|LBY|1977}} [[1982 Africa Cup of Nations|1982]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[1984 Africa Cup of Nations|1984]]
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[1986 Africa Cup of Nations|1986]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[1988 Africa Cup of Nations|1988]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ALG}} [[1990 Africa Cup of Nations|1990]]
|colspan=8|''Did not enter''
|-
|colspan=9|Played as '''{{fb|CGO|name=Republic of the Congo}}'''
|-
|{{flagicon|SEN}} [[1992 Africa Cup of Nations|1992]]
|Quarter-finals
|5th
|3
|0
|2
|1
|2
|3
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN|1959}} [[1994 Africa Cup of Nations|1994]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=3|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[1996 Africa Cup of Nations|1996]]
|-
|{{flagicon|BFA}} [[1998 Africa Cup of Nations|1998]]
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} {{flagicon|NGA}} [[2000 Africa Cup of Nations|2000]]
|Group stage
|11th
|3
|0
|1
|2
|0
|2
|-
|{{flagicon|MLI}} [[2002 Africa Cup of Nations|2002]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=7|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|TUN}} [[2004 Africa Cup of Nations|2004]]
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[2006 Africa Cup of Nations|2006]]
|-
|{{flagicon|GHA}} [[2008 Africa Cup of Nations|2008]]
|-
|{{flagicon|ANG}} [[2010 Africa Cup of Nations|2010]]
|-
|{{flagicon|GAB}} {{flagicon|EQG}} [[2012 Africa Cup of Nations|2012]]
|-
|{{flagicon|RSA}} [[2013 Africa Cup of Nations|2013]]
|-
|{{flagicon|EQG}} [[2015 Africa Cup of Nations|2015]]
|Quarter-finals
|5th
|4
|2
|1
|1
|6
|6
|-
|{{flagicon|GAB}} [[2017 Africa Cup of Nations|2017]]
|colspan=8 rowspan=5|''Did not qualify''
|-
|{{flagicon|EGY}} [[2019 Africa Cup of Nations|2019]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CMR}} [[2021 Africa Cup of Nations|2021]]
|-
|{{flagicon|CIV}} [[2023 Africa Cup of Nations|2023]]
|-
|{{flagicon|MAR}} [[2025 Africa Cup of Nations|2025]]
|-
|{{flagicon|KEN}} {{flagicon|TAN}} {{flagicon|UGA}} [[2027 Africa Cup of Nations|2027]]
| colspan="8" rowspan="2" |''To be determined''
|-
|{{flagicon|}} [[2028 Africa Cup of Nations|2028]]
|-
!Total
!1 Title
!7/35
!26
!7
!7
!12
!27
!40
|}

=== African Games ===
{{See also|Congo national under-23 football team}}
{{See also|Congo national under-20 football team}}
{| class="wikitable" style="text-align: center;"
|-
!colspan=9|[[Football at the African Games|African Games]] record
|-
!Year||Result||Pld||W||D||L||GF||GA
|-
|-bgcolor=gold
|{{flagicon|Congo}} [[Football at the 1965 All-Africa Games|1965]]||Gold||'''5'''||'''3'''||'''1'''||'''1'''||'''11'''||'''5'''
|-
|{{flagicon|Nigeria}} [[Football at the 1973 All-Africa Games|1973]]||6th||'''3'''||'''1'''||'''0'''||'''2'''||'''5'''||'''8'''
|-
!'''Total'''||'''2/4'''||'''8'''||'''4'''||'''1'''||'''3'''||'''16'''||'''13'''
|}

==Honours==
===Continental===
* '''[[Africa Cup of Nations|CAF African Cup of Nations]]'''
** [[File:Coppa Africa.svg|22px]] '''Champions (1)''': [[1972 African Cup of Nations|1972]]
* '''[[Football at the African Games|African Games]]'''<sup>'''1'''</sup>
** [[File:Gold medal africa.svg|15px]] '''Gold medal (1)''': [[Football at the 1965 All-Africa Games|1965]]

===Regional===
* '''[[CEMAC Cup]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (2)''': 2007, 2010
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (2): 2008, 2014
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (2): 2003, 2013
* '''[[UDEAC Championship]]'''
** {{Gold1}} '''Champions (1)''': 1990
** {{Silver2}} Runners-up (2): 1984, 1985
** {{Bronze3}} Third place (2): 1986, 1988
* '''[[Football at the Central African Games|Central African Games]]'''
** {{Silver2}} Silver medal (2): 1976, 1981
** {{Bronze3}} Bronze medal (1): 1987

===Summary===
{| class="wikitable" width=30% style="font-size:90%; text-align:center;"
|-
!Competition !!{{gold1}} !!{{silver2}} !!{{bronze3}} !!Total
|-
|align=left|[[African Cup of Nations|CAF African Cup of Nations]]
|1||0||0||1
|-
!Total !!1 !!0 !!0 !!1
|}
;Notes
# Competition organized by [[Association of National Olympic Committees of Africa|ANOCA]], officially not recognized by FIFA.

== Notes ==
{{notelist}} 

== References ==
{{Reflist}}

== External links ==
{{Commons category}}
* {{Official website}}
* {{facebook|fecofootcg}}
* [https://www.cafonline.com/member-associations/cgo/ Congo] at [[Confederation of African Football|CAF]]
* [https://www.fifa.com/about-fifa/associations/CGO Congo] at [[FIFA]]

{{Africa Cup of Nations Winners}}
{{Congo national football team}}
{{Navboxes
|title=Congo Africa Cup of Nations squads
|bg=#DC241F
|fg=#FBDE4A
|bordercolor=#009543
|list1=
{{Congo squad 1992 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Congo squad 2000 African Cup of Nations}}
{{Congo squad 2015 Africa Cup of Nations}}
}}
{{Football in the Republic of the Congo}}
{{CAF teams}}
{{National sports teams of the Republic of the Congo}}

[[Category:Congo national football team| ]]
[[Category:African national association football teams]]
[[Category:Africa Cup of Nations–winning countries|C]]
